knownIssues: comfy_kitchen QTensor.clone() segfaults on .to('xpu') when the FP8 quantized text encoder is moved to Intel XPU directly.
fp8 -> bf16 dequant roughly doubles activation memory; pair with a VRAM gate.
Without the patch, ComfyUI may appear to import and register CLIPLoader fine but segfault on first inference, so 'node registered' evidence is not sufficient.

workarounds.action: Apply xpu-bug-investigation/0001-xpu-fp8-fallback-dequantize-before-move-to-xpu.patch to comfy/ops.py so _quantized_apply dequantizes FP8 tensors to bf16 before moving to XPU.
workarounds.rationale: Avoids the QTensor.clone() path that triggers the segfault; bf16 tensors move cleanly to XPU.
workarounds.tradeoff: Doubles activation memory (fp8 -> bf16). Requires ~2x VRAM headroom for the text encoder; not viable on small cards.

workarounds.action: If VRAM headroom is insufficient, set CLIPLoader device=cpu via runtime-policy JSON and keep the text encoder on CPU.
workarounds.rationale: Sidesteps the segfault entirely; CPU execution of the TE is correct, just slower.
workarounds.tradeoff: First-prompt latency increases by seconds-to-tens-of-seconds depending on prompt length. Throughput after cache is unaffected.

workarounds.action: If the checkpoint contains unused visual/lm_head buckets (common for text-only fine-tunes of qwen2.5-vl), strip them to a *_text_only.safetensors file and point CLIPLoader at the stripped file.
workarounds.rationale: Smaller file loads faster, uses less RAM during dequant, and avoids loading buckets the text-only ComfyUI workflow never touches.
workarounds.tradeoff: One-time preprocessing step; the stripped file must be referenced consistently thereafter.

retireCondition: comfy_kitchen >= 0.3.0 fixes QTensor.clone() segfault so the dequant-before-move patch becomes a no-op.
